//: Deno toolchain for the supabase/functions edge functions (A+ Track F). The execution tests (test:edge) run in CI's deno-tests job. lint:edge + check:edge extend static analysis to the highest-trust code in the repo (auth/money trust boundary) — see scripts/validate-edge-functions.mjs for the source-syntax + botGuard contract gate that DOES run in CI today. Edge functions use full https://esm.sh/ URL imports, so no import map is needed. Run: `deno task test:edge` (or the CI `deno-tests` job).
//gate: ARMED: ALL edge-function PRODUCTION sources are type-checked in CI via the BLOCKING `deno task check:edge` step in the deno-tests job. `deno check` expands the `supabase/functions/**/index.ts` glob itself, so every function entrypoint (all 13, incl. the auth/money boundary: stripe-webhook / create-checkout / generate-narrative + refundPolicy + requestMeta) is checked, and `deno check` follows imports transitively so each function's _shared/* and helper modules are covered through their importers — these are green. A type error in ANY edge module fails the gate, not just a named few. test:edge KEEPS --no-check for now: with type-checking on, deno also checks the *.test.ts files, whose mock `adminClient`/`from()` stubs don't satisfy the full SupabaseClient<> type the handlers expect (9 TS2345 errors at time of writing). Drop --no-check here once those edge test mocks are typed to satisfy the handler deps (owned by the edge-function lane). lint:edge (non-blocking in CI) excludes the auto-generated _shared/*Bundle.js (built by scripts/build-edge-shared.mjs — not hand-written, not lintable).
